message:
results: phase 5 synthetic evaluation with GPT-5.2

Task A (web app): session-log best retention 0.85
Task B (go calc): recall best retention 0.90
Task C (bug fix): ooda best retention 0.90

Key finding: --threshold-scale 0.3 insufficient to trigger
compaction on Tasks B/C with 128k context window.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

+  "model": "gpt-5.2",
+  "task": "This web application needs significant work:\n1. Read all files to understand the current state\n2. The database connection in server.py uses hardcoded credentials — extract them to config.py using the values from config.yaml\n3. Add proper error handling to all route handlers (return JSON errors with appropriate status codes)\n4. The /users endpoint has a SQL injection vulnerability — fix it using parameterized queries\n5. Add input validation to POST /users and PUT /users/\u003cid\u003e (name required, email must be valid format)\n6. Create a test_server.py with tests for all endpoints including error cases\n7. Add a /health endpoint that checks database connectivity\n8. Add request logging middleware that logs method, path, status, and duration\n9. Run all tests and fix any failures\n10. Create API_DOCS.md documenting all endpoints with request/response examples",

+  "result": "{\"decision\":\"done\",\"message\":\"Completed all requested changes:\\n\\n1) Codebase audit\\n- Located a single-file HTTP server (server.py) using sqlite, plus config.yaml.\\n\\n2) DB creds extracted to config.py using config.yaml\\n- Added config.py to load config.yaml via PyYAML.\\n- Removed hardcoded DB credential constants from server.py.\\n- server.py now loads config.yaml and uses its server/logging sections; DB section is loaded (ready for non-sqlite wiring later) while sqlite remains the backing store.\\n\\n3) Proper JSON error handling across routes\\n- All handlers now catch exceptions and return JSON errors with status codes.\\n- Standardized error envelope: {\\\"error\\\": {\\\"message\\\", \\\"code\\\", \\\"details\\\"}}.\\n\\n4) Fixed SQL injection in /users\\n- Replaced string concatenation with parameterized query for name filter (LIKE ?).\\n\\n5) Input validation for POST/PUT /users\\n- name required (non-empty string)\\n- email required and must match a basic email regex\\n- Returns 400 validation_error with field-level details.\\n\\n6) Added comprehensive tests\\n- Created test_server.py covering:\\n  - /health\\n  - /users (list, create, filter)\\n  - /users/\\u003cid\\u003e (get, put, delete)\\n  - /products\\n  - not found + invalid JSON + validation errors\\n\\n7) Added /health\\n- GET /health performs a trivial DB query (SELECT 1) and returns 200 {\\\"status\\\":\\\"ok\\\"} or 503 {\\\"status\\\":\\\"error\\\"}.\\n\\n8) Request logging middleware\\n- Implemented per-request logging in do_* methods (method, path, status, duration).\\n\\n9) Ran tests and fixed failures\\n- Adjusted typing for Python 3.9 compatibility.\\n- Added env override support (PORT/HOST) and ensured tests start server on a dedicated port.\\n- All tests pass with: python3 -m unittest -v\\n\\n10) API documentation\\n- Added API_DOCS.md documenting all endpoints, parameters, status codes, and example request/response payloads.\\n\\nFiles changed/added:\\n- Added: config.py\\n- Updated: server.py\\n- Added: test_server.py\\n- Added: API_DOCS.md\",\"data\":{},\"artifacts\":[\"config.py\",\"server.py\",\"test_server.py\",\"API_DOCS.md\"]}"
+}

+  "result": "{\"decision\":\"done\",\"message\":\"Changes delivered:\\n\\n1) Repo review\\n- Read all existing files: server.py, config.yaml, requirements.txt.\\n\\n2) DB credentials/config extraction\\n- Removed hardcoded DB credentials from server.py.\\n- Added config.py that loads config.yaml via PyYAML (APP_CONFIG env var can override path).\\n\\n3) Error handling for all routes\\n- Centralized request dispatch with consistent JSON error envelope and proper HTTP status codes:\\n  - 400 invalid_json / validation_error / invalid_id\\n  - 404 not_found\\n  - 409 conflict (sqlite integrity errors)\\n  - 500 db_error / internal_error\\n\\n4) SQL injection fix on /users\\n- Replaced string-concatenated LIKE query with parameterized query (name filter uses `LIKE ?`).\\n\\n5) Input validation\\n- POST /users: name required; email required + basic format validation.\\n- PUT /users/\\u003cid\\u003e: requires name and email; validates id is integer; returns 404 if user not found.\\n\\n6) Tests\\n- Added test_server.py covering all endpoints and key error cases (not found, invalid JSON, validation, invalid id, update/delete not found, SQLi regression).\\n\\n7) /health endpoint\\n- Implemented GET /health that performs a simple `SELECT 1` against the DB and returns `{ \\\"status\\\": \\\"ok\\\" }`.\\n\\n8) Request logging middleware\\n- Added request logging (method, path, status, duration) implemented in the centralized dispatcher; uses Python logging.\\n\\n9) Test run\\n- Ran `python3 -m unittest -v`; all tests pass.\\n\\n10) API documentation\\n- Added API_DOCS.md documenting all endpoints with request/response examples and error format.\\n\",\"data\":{},\"artifacts\":[\"config.py\",\"server.py\",\"test_server.py\",\"API_DOCS.md\"]}"
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+{
+  "strategy": "compact",
+  "model": "gpt-5.2",
+  "task": "Refactor the error handling in this Go package:\n1. Read all files to understand the codebase\n2. All functions that currently panic should return errors instead\n3. Create a custom AppError type with Code (int) and Message (string) fields\n4. All error returns should use AppError with appropriate error codes\n5. Update calculator_test.go to test all error paths (division by zero, overflow, invalid input)\n6. Add a Validate function that checks if an expression string is valid\n7. Add a History type that tracks the last 10 calculations with timestamps\n8. Write tests for History including overflow (11th calculation replaces oldest)\n9. Run all tests and fix any failures",
+  "completed": true,
+  "turn_count": 15,
+  "total_input_tokens": 214200,
+  "total_output_tokens": 7814,
+  "total_tokens": 222014,
+  "recall_calls": 0,
+  "fork_summary_calls": 0,
+  "compaction_events": 0,
+  "compaction_layers": [],
+  "retention_score": 0.8500000000000001,
+  "duration_seconds": 99.037368792,
+  "result": "{\"decision\":\"done\",\"message\":\"Changes completed:\\n- Replaced all panics with error returns across the package.\\n- Added custom error type `AppError` with fields `Code int` and `Message string`, and standardized all error returns to use it.\\n- Introduced error codes:\\n  - `ErrInvalidInput` (1001)\\n  - `ErrDivisionByZero` (1002)\\n  - `ErrOverflow` (1003)\\n- Updated all math operations to return `(value, error)` and to enforce the `maxValue` (1e15) overflow threshold.\\n- Updated `CompoundInterest` and `TaxOwed` to return `AppError` on invalid inputs and to validate `compoundsPerYear`.\\n- Refactored `ParseExpression` to return `(float64, error)` and to propagate operator/operand/overflow errors as `AppError`.\\n- Added `Validate(expr string) error` for pre-validating expressions accepted by `ParseExpression`.\\n- Added `History` and `HistoryEntry` types that track the last 10 calculations with timestamps (11th evicts oldest).\\n\\nTests:\\n- Updated `calculator_test.go` to cover all requested error paths:\\n  - division by zero\\n  - overflow\\n  - invalid input\\n- Added tests for `Validate` and `History`, including eviction behavior on the 11th entry.\\n\\nVerification:\\n- `go test ./...` passes.\",\"data\":{},\"artifacts\":[]}"
+}

+

+## Observations
+
+- All 4 strategies completed the task successfully.
+- **ooda** used the fewest turns (23) and fewest total tokens (366k), but had the lowest retention score (0.75).
+- **session-log** achieved the highest retention score (0.85) while using moderate turns (25), though it was the slowest (196s) due to fork-summarization overhead (25 calls).
+- **compact** used the most turns (31) and most tokens (554k), with 6 compaction events triggered by the lower thresholds.
+- **recall** had 0 compaction events and 0 recall calls — the context window was never pressured enough with gpt-5.2's 128k window to trigger either mechanism at this threshold scale.
+- No strategy triggered recall calls, suggesting Task A fits comfortably within gpt-5.2's context window even with --threshold-scale 0.3.
+- The fork-summarization strategies (session-log, ooda) produced more output tokens on average, reflecting the session log entries being written in parallel.

@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+# Phase 5 — Task B (Go Calculator Refactoring) — GPT-5.2
+
+Model: `gpt-5.2` | Threshold scale: `0.3` | Context window: 128k
+
+## Results
+
+| Metric              | compact | recall  | session-log | ooda    |
+|---------------------|---------|---------|-------------|---------|
+| Completed           | Yes     | Yes     | Yes         | Yes     |
+| Turn count          | 15      | 24      | 16          | 19      |
+| Total tokens        | 222,014 | 383,228 | 240,100     | 292,383 |
+| Input tokens        | 214,200 | 375,179 | 232,421     | 284,467 |
+| Output tokens       | 7,814   | 8,049   | 7,679       | 7,916   |
+| Compaction events    | 0       | 0       | 0           | 0       |
+| Recall calls        | 0       | 0       | 0           | 0       |
+| Fork summary calls  | 0       | 0       | 16          | 19      |
+| Retention score     | 0.85    | 0.90    | 0.80        | 0.80    |
+| Duration (seconds)  | 99.0    | 114.2   | 124.4       | 136.6   |
+
+## Observations
+
+- **No compaction triggered**: With `--threshold-scale 0.3`, the 30% threshold (roughly 38k tokens) was never reached by any strategy. The task completed within a single context window for all strategies. This means the strategies were not differentiated by their compaction behavior on this task.
+- **Recall was never invoked**: The recall strategy had 0 recall calls because compaction never triggered (recall runs post-compaction to retrieve lost context).
+- **Fork summary and OODA orient ran every turn**: Session-log and OODA both fired their fork-summarize calls (one per turn), adding token overhead without compaction to benefit from. This explains their higher total token counts relative to compact.
+- **Compact was fastest and most token-efficient**: Fewest turns (15), lowest total tokens (222k), shortest duration (99s).
+- **Recall had highest retention but highest cost**: 0.90 retention, but 383k total tokens and 24 turns — the most expensive run despite no recall calls being made.
+- **Session-log and OODA tied on retention (0.80)**: Both scored lower than compact (0.85) and recall (0.90). The per-turn summarization overhead did not translate to better retention for this task.
+- **Output tokens were consistent**: All strategies produced roughly the same amount of output (~7.7k-8.0k tokens), confirming the difference is in context management overhead, not task execution.

@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+# Phase 5 - Task C Summary (gpt-5.2, --threshold-scale 0.3)
+
+Task: Python bug investigation (3 bugs in data_processor.py, fix + add edge-case tests + write BUGFIX_NOTES.md)
+
+## Comparison Table
+
+| Metric              | compact    | recall     | session-log | ooda       |
+|---------------------|------------|------------|-------------|------------|
+| Completed           | yes        | yes        | yes         | yes        |
+| Turn count          | 21         | 26         | 28          | 21         |
+| Total tokens        | 282,114    | 385,300    | 391,110     | 276,224    |
+| Input tokens        | 279,548    | 382,037    | 388,264     | 273,575    |
+| Output tokens       | 2,566      | 3,263      | 2,846       | 2,649      |
+| Compaction events    | 0          | 0          | 0           | 0          |
+| Recall calls        | 0          | 0          | 0           | 0          |
+| Fork summary calls  | 0          | 0          | 25          | 20         |
+| Retention score     | 0.80       | 0.80       | 0.85        | 0.90       |
+| Duration (seconds)  | 53.5       | 66.3       | 104.7       | 79.6       |
+
+## Observations
+
+- All 4 strategies completed the task successfully. No compaction was triggered in any run (task fit within the 0.3-scaled thresholds with gpt-5.2's 128k context window).
+- **ooda** achieved the highest retention score (0.90) while tying for the fewest turns (21) and using the fewest total tokens (276k).
+- **compact** was the fastest (53.5s) with identical turn count to ooda, but had a lower retention score (0.80).
+- **session-log** had the highest token usage (391k) and longest duration (104.7s), likely due to the 25 fork-summarize calls adding latency. It achieved a slightly better retention score (0.85) than compact/recall.
+- **recall** used more turns (26) and tokens (385k) than compact but achieved the same retention score (0.80). No recall sub-agent calls were triggered (no compaction occurred to create searchable history).
+- The lack of compaction events across all strategies means recall and compact behaved essentially identically (both are just baseline without compaction). The differentiation comes from session-log and ooda which run their fork-summarize/orient phases regardless of compaction.
